qa_pairs.question: What is the Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Canadian Common CV (CCV) is a Canadian Institutes of Health Research-funded (CIHR) software system designed to standardize CV submissions to granting agencies for funding competitions.

qa_pairs.question: Which major funding agencies use the Canadian Common CV?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCV is currently used by NSERC, SSHRC, NCE, CIHR itself, and the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the Alliance require faculty users to submit a CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance requires faculty users to submit a CCV for renewing their Alliance account and for applying for the Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: For whom is submitting a CCV mandatory when renewing an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Submitting a CCV is mandatory for all users with Faculty and Adjunct Faculty roles, except for Emeritus Professors, Librarians, and Retired Faculty.

qa_pairs.question: For whom is submitting a CCV optional when renewing an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Submitting a CCV is optional for Emeritus Professors, Librarians, and Retired Faculty when renewing their Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to submit an updated CCV for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: An updated CCV is required for the Principal Investigator (PI) and ALL the co-PIs of a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Can Emeritus Professors, Retired Faculty, and Librarians choose not to submit a CCV for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: No, even if it's optional for renewing their account, submission of the CCV is mandatory for ALL PIs and Co-PIs of a RAC application, including Emeritus Professors, Retired Faculty, and Librarians if they hold those roles.

qa_pairs.question: How can Principal Investigators (PIs) update their CCV at any time?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can update their CCV at any time by going to 'My Account → View Reporting' on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I forget my CCV username or password, or need technical support for accessing https://ccv-cvc.ca/?
qa_pairs.answer: You should contact the CCV helpdesk directly by email at support@cihr-irsc.gc.ca or by phone at 613-954-1968 or 1-888-603-4178.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should I contact for specific questions about the information required in the Alliance CCV template or issues submitting my CCV to the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: You should contact support@tech.alliancecan.ca for specific questions about the information required in the Alliance CCV template or issues submitting your CCV to the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: For whom does the Alliance provide CCV support?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance provides support only for Alliance account holders that need to submit a CCV to renew an account or apply for RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I am not an Alliance account holder and need CCV support?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are not an Alliance account holder, you should contact the agency to which you are trying to submit a CCV directly.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to submit a CCV to renew their Alliance account and submit an application to Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCV is mandatory for all university Faculty that want to renew their Alliance account and submit an application to any of our Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: Who is able to submit applications to the Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Only users with active CCDB accounts will be able to submit applications to the RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to register for a new CCV account?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to go to https://ccv-cvc.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: How do you start the registration process on the CCV website?
qa_pairs.answer: After going to https://ccv-cvc.ca/ and selecting your preferred language, click on the 'Login' button, then click on the 'Register' button at the bottom of the screen.

qa_pairs.question: What information is important to include when filling out the CCV registration form?
qa_pairs.answer: When filling out the form, it is important to enter a valid email address.

qa_pairs.question: What are the final steps to complete CCV account registration?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to select your password and security questions, click on the 'I'm not a robot box', click on the 'Submit' button, follow instructions in the confirmation email, and then log in to the CCV website.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to items added or updated while filling out an Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Any items you add or update while filling out an Alliance CCV will be added to your generic CCV for future use, such as for submitting to other granting agencies.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step to submit an Alliance CCV after logging into the CCV site?
qa_pairs.answer: In the top menu bar, select 'CV', and then in the drop-down list, select 'Funding.'

qa_pairs.question: Which 'Funding Source' should be selected when submitting an Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: You should select 'Digital Research Alliance of Canada' in the 'Funding Source' field.

qa_pairs.question: Which 'CV Type' should be selected for an Alliance CCV submission?
qa_pairs.answer: You should select 'Alliance' in the 'CV Type' field.

qa_pairs.question: How do you display the Alliance-specific CCV fields after selecting the funding source and CV type?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on the 'Load' button at the top of the window to display the list of CCV fields required by the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: What does a green checkmark indicate in the Alliance CCV submission list?
qa_pairs.answer: A green checkmark indicates that the information provided meets Alliance’s criteria for submission.

qa_pairs.question: What does a red x-mark indicate in the Alliance CCV submission list?
qa_pairs.answer: A red x-mark indicates that content is missing.

qa_pairs.question: How do you edit missing or incorrectly filled information in the Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on the pencil icon to edit the information; the CCV interface will inform you which fields are missing or filled out incorrectly.

qa_pairs.question: How can you preview your Alliance CCV submission?
qa_pairs.answer: You can preview your CCV submission by clicking on the 'Preview' button.

qa_pairs.question: What is the final step to begin the Alliance CCV submission process?
qa_pairs.answer: To begin the submission process, click on the 'Submit' button.

qa_pairs.question: What critical piece of information must be noted after submitting an Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: You must make note of the 'confirmation number' assigned upon submission.

qa_pairs.question: Why is the confirmation number important for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: It is mandatory to enter this number on the CCDB to link your RAC application with your CCV submission.

qa_pairs.question: Can confirmation numbers from other CCV templates (e.g., NSERC, CIHR) be used for Alliance RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: No, only confirmation numbers for CCVs submitted using the 'Alliance' template will be accepted.
